paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number
summary: Problem 12 asks for the exact value of $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(\vec{K}_n)$; the source paper only establishes the doubly-exponential upper bound $4^{n^2-2n+1}(n-1)+1$. The most relevant follow-up, Gishboliner–Steiner–Szabó (2020, arXiv:2008.09888), proves $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(F)=v(F)$ for octus digraphs and orientations of cactus graphs, but the complete digraph $\vec{K}_n$ falls outside these classes and the exact value remains unknown. No paper resolving Problem 12 was found in the indexed literature.

since_posted.title: Dichromatic number and forced subdivisions
since_posted.authors: Lior Gishboliner, Raphael Steiner, Tibor Szabó
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Proves $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(F)=v(F)$ for every octus digraph $F$ and for every orientation of a cactus graph; $\vec{K}_n$ is not in these classes, so the exact value of $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(\vec{K}_n)$ is not settled.

since_posted.title: Oriented cycles in digraphs of large outdegree
since_posted.authors: Lior Gishboliner, Raphael Steiner, Tibor Szabó
since_posted.claim: Proves that every digraph with minimum out-degree at least $K(\ell)$ contains a subdivision of every orientation of a cycle of length $\ell$; addresses $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}$ for oriented cycles rather than $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(\vec{K}_n)$.

internal_refs_verified.note: Confirmed: proves the oriented-cycle conjecture (mader_{delta+} variant, Theorem 6) but does not address mader_chi(K_n). Internal-ref claims about kappa'-maderian and kappa-maderian pertain to a different pair of questions in the source paper, not Problem 12.

Confirmed: proves mader_chi(F)=v(F) for octus digraphs and cactus orientations (Theorem 7, Corollary 8), published in JCTB. The internal-ref claim that this 'fully resolves the conjecture' is overstated for Problem 12: K_n is not an octus digraph or cactus orientation, so the exact value of mader_chi(K_n) remains open.
Confirmed: 'Chordal directed graphs are not chi-bounded' by Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos; cites the source paper as broader context only, no direct progress on Problem 12.

search_queries: mader dichromatic number complete digraph K_n subdivision 1610.00876
mader_chi K_n complete digraph dichromatic subdivision forced
notes: The search also surfaced arXiv:2101.04590 (Mészáros–Steiner, 2021, 'Complete minors in digraphs with given dichromatic number'), which improves bounds on forced complete *minors* under dichromatic number; minors are weaker than subdivisions and this paper does not resolve Problem 12. No paper giving the exact value of mader_chi(K_n) was found.
